_comment.1: Open-loop plant Bode of the y/tip axis: command -> FSM -> camera -> CoM.
_comment.2: SOFTWARE injection: bode_plot replaces the y element with a sine each
_comment.3: iteration, and the y piplate_bridge writes offset + sine*scale to the DAC
_comment.4: every cycle. The bias (1.9368 V) therefore comes from a real per-iteration
_comment.5: DAQ write, not the hardware FG's fg_offset (which does not actually bias
_comment.6: the channel). No PID is in the loop, so the command IS the sine = open loop.
_comment.7: Pipeline: camera -> center_of_mass -> bode_plot(replace y) -> drive y -> park x.
_comment.8: Phase is measured from wall-clock time, so the freq axis is exact.
_comment.9: Read the -180 deg crossing off fsm815_bode_y.pdf: that frequency is where
_comment.10: the loop self-oscillates, and the gain there sets max py. Magnitude is now
_comment.11: calibrated (CoM units per command unit) since amplitude is the real drive.
_comment.12: Raise amplitude for SNR but keep the spot on the 72x72 sensor.

pipeline._comment: Replace the y/tip element (0) with the swept sine; read CoM y as response
Drive y/tip: DAC ch1, writes offset(1.9368V) + sine*scale every iteration
Park x/tilt at its center voltage (scale 0) to hold the spot on-sensor
